title: aphora drummondii
intro: Aphora drummondii, also known as Prairie Turnip or Drummond's Cress, is a perennial plant native to North America, belonging to the Brassicaceae family. Its medicinal properties include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Aphora drummondii include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support immune function. The plant can be prepared as an herbal remedy by making a tea from its leaves and roots, or by using it in ointments and salves for topical application.
